ques: Find a report on the BBC News website about recent developments in renewable energy technologies in the UK.
Read the latest health-related news article published on BBC News and summarize the key points discussed.
Read the latest article regarding the environmental impacts of deforestation published within the last two days.
Check the leaderboard for Golf's DP World Tour in the SPORT section, what was the name of the most recent tournament, and how many teams have a Total of -10 strokes.
Find the latest article regarding the economic implications of climate change in Europe as reported by BBC News and summarize the central points.
Find the article "What is climate change? A really simple guide" and use it to answer what human activities are causing climate change.
Find the top story from BBC News in the technology section for today.
Find a AI-related story under Technology of Business. What is in the first picture in the story?
Get a brief overview of the economic implications of the UK's latest trade deal posted on BBC News and the date when the article was published.
Find out which musician made the headlines in Music News.
Identify the main headlines covering the UK's plan to tackle climate change on BBC News.
Find out how many teams are in the Scottish Premiership of the Football Tournament and when did the Hibernian team's most recent match start?
Find a picture in the travel section that contains food, tell me what the food is called and what region it comes from.
Search for recent news related to Trump and summarize the main points.
Find a news article on BBC News about the impact of the recent tech industry layoffs on the global economy. Summarize the key points and the name of the author, and provide the date of publication.
What does the current headline in Natural Wonders tell about.
Identify the most recent development or update in Brexit negotiations as reported on BBC News and report the key points and any stated impacts on European economies.
How many War related sections are currently in BBC News.
Visit BBC News Audio, What are the best PodCasts for 2023? List 2 of them.
Visit the Athletics calendar for the date of the next earliest game.
Find the latest article in the Green Living section on BBC News and provide a summary of its main points.
Identify the top headline in the World News section on BBC News and describe the region it is related to.
Determine the current top business story on BBC News and give a brief overview of its economic implications.
Identify the latest health-related news on BBC News and summarize the main findings or recommendations.
Search the latest article about space exploration on BBC News and summarize its key points.
Find the most recent sports analysis article on BBC News related to the English Premier League and summarize its key insights.
Locate the latest report on BBC News about the impact of recent natural disasters in Asia and summarize the key points and areas affected.
Find the most recent article on BBC News about archaeological discoveries and summarize the main findings and their significance.
Find the Market Data section on BBC News and tell me which company the data comes from.
Visit BBC News Audio and find out which podcast episode is currently featured as the "New Releases".
In the Culture section, identify the latest film release reviewed and provide a brief summary of the review.
Check the Sports section for the result of the most recent Manchester United football match.
Find the artificial intelligence section, what is the top headline at this time, and which companies are involved?
In the World News section, find the latest war situations of Middle East and provide a brief summary.
Find The SpeciaList section in Travel and browse the page to see which cities are mentioned.
In the Asia section, browse and identify the most recent report about technological advancements and summarize its content.
Look up recent articles in the Africa news section in World, summarize what topics most of these news are about
Identify the latest book review featured in the Culture section and provide the title and author of the book.
Find news related to the storm in Weather section and indicate where and when the severe weather occurred.
Check the Horse Racing results in Sport section, browse all the games that took place yesterday and see which one had the highest number of runners.
Read and summarise a recent story on BBC News about people being injured or killed in wars.

Find Golf in BBC News, check the Leaderboard at this point in Women's Majors and count which country has the most players in the top 20? Which player has the best score amongst the Australian players and in what place.
Use the search box at the top of BBC News to search for the keyword 'quantum' and report how many articles are listed on the first page of results.
Search BBC News for the term 'NHS' and open the first result. What section is the article filed under?
Search BBC News for 'Starlink' and summarise the headline and lead paragraph of the first matching article.
Search BBC News for 'PCOS'. Open the top result and report the article's published date and reading time.
Search BBC News for the keyword 'Brexit' and report how many results are returned on the search page.
Search BBC News for 'OpenAI' and list the headlines of the top three results.
Search BBC News for 'climate change' and from the results, find one article whose subsection is 'Science & Environment'. Give its headline.
Search BBC News for 'fintech' and report the headline of the first result and which World region (Africa/Asia/Europe/etc.) it belongs to.
Use the BBC News search to find an article about 'weight-loss jab' and report the medication's status with the NHS.
Search BBC News for 'Formula 1' and open the most-viewed result. What is the article's author byline?
Search BBC News for 'wildfire' and from the results pick the article from the World section. Summarise its key facts in two sentences.
Search BBC News for 'Wimbledon' and report the headline of the most recent matching article.
Search BBC News for 'recession' and report whether any results appear in the Business section, and how many there are.
Search BBC News for 'spaceflight' and list the first two headlines and their published dates.
Search BBC News for 'inflation' and from the first page of results, count how many articles are in the Business section.
Navigate to the Africa section under World on BBC News and list the headlines of the first three articles.
Navigate to the Asia section under World on BBC News and report the headline of the top story.
Navigate to the Europe section under World on BBC News and find an article about EU policy. Summarise its main argument.
Navigate to the Middle East section under World on BBC News and report how many articles are listed on the first page.
Navigate to the US & Canada section under World on BBC News and list the top three headlines.
Navigate to the Latin America section under World on BBC News and identify the country mentioned in the top headline.
Go to the Scotland section under UK on BBC News and report the headline of the most recent article and its published date.
Go to the Wales section under UK on BBC News and list the top three headlines.
Go to the Northern Ireland section under UK on BBC News and report how many articles are filed in the section.
Go to the England section under UK on BBC News and find an article tagged with the Education subsection. Report its headline.
From BBC News, navigate to Business > Economy and summarise the lead paragraph of the top story.
From BBC News, navigate to Business > Companies and identify which company is named in the top headline.
From BBC News, navigate to Business > Your Money and report the headline and reading time of the first article.
Navigate to the Innovation section on BBC News and open the Artificial Intelligence sub-section. List the top three headlines.
Navigate to the Health section on BBC News and report the byline (author) of the top story.
Navigate to the Science section on BBC News and report which of the top three stories mentions space or astronomy.
Open the Politics section on BBC News and report the headlines of the top two articles plus their published times.
Open the Entertainment section on BBC News, find an article about a film or music release, and report its headline and the artist or film it covers.
Open the Arts section on BBC News and list the headlines of the top two stories.
Open the Earth section on BBC News, navigate to Green Living, and report the headline of the top story.
Open the Travel section on BBC News and report the title of the sub-section that contains travel destination features.
Open the Live page on BBC News and report how many live stream entries are currently listed.
Open the Latest page on BBC News and list the section labels (e.g. World, Business) shown on the first five articles.
Visit the article 'Google Gemini gets real-time video understanding in latest update' on BBC News and report its published date and word count.
Open any article in the Technology section on BBC News and report the reading time shown at the top of the article.
Open the article 'Quantum computing milestone: IBM demonstrates error-corrected logic qubits' on BBC News and summarise its first paragraph in your own words.
Open the most-viewed Business article on BBC News and report the view count shown on the article page.
Open any article tagged with the 'AI' topic on BBC News and list every topic tag shown at the bottom of the article.
Navigate to the Sport > Football section on BBC News and report the top headline and its published date.
Navigate to the Sport > Tennis section on BBC News and list the top three article headlines.
Navigate to the Sport > Cricket section on BBC News and report how many articles are listed on the first page.
Navigate to the Sport > Rugby Union section on BBC News and report the headline of the most recent article.
Navigate to the Sport > Golf section on BBC News and report the headline of an article about a recent tournament.
Navigate to the Sport > Athletics section on BBC News and list two top headlines.
Browse the Sport > Horse Racing section on BBC News and report whether the top story mentions a specific race meeting.
Visit the BBC News About page and report the year the BBC was founded as stated on that page.
Visit the BBC News Help page and list two of the help topics linked from the page.
Visit the BBC News Contact page and report the support email address shown on the page.
Open the BBC News Privacy page and report the date the privacy policy was last updated.
Open the BBC News Terms of Use page and report the section heading that explains acceptable use.
Open the BBC News Cookies page and list two categories of cookies described on the page.
Open the BBC News Accessibility page and report the contact channel offered for users with accessibility needs.
Register a new BBC News account using the email reader1@test.com, username reader1, full name Reader One and a password of your choice. After signing up, confirm you have landed on the account page.
Sign in to BBC News with email alice.j@test.com and password TestPass123!. After signing in, report the full name displayed on the account page.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, open the account edit page, change the location field to 'Manchester, UK' and save. Confirm the new location is shown on the account page.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, change the password to NewPass456! using the change-password form, then sign out and sign back in with the new password.
Sign in to BBC News as demo@bbcnews.local / demodemo and visit your account page. Report the location and bio shown for this user.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123! and open the reading history page. Report how many articles are listed there.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123! and clear your reading history using the 'Clear history' button. Verify the list becomes empty.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, then bookmark the top article from the Technology section. Confirm the article appears on the Bookmarks page.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, open the Bookmarks page and report how many bookmarks are listed.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, bookmark any Health article, then remove it from the Bookmarks page. Confirm the bookmark count returns to its original value.
Sign in to BBC News as david.k@test.com / TestPass123! and bookmark the top three Sport articles. Confirm all three appear on the Bookmarks page in the order you added them.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123! and open the Reading List. Report the folder names that currently contain at least one item.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, add the top Science article to your Reading List in the 'Work' folder.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123! and create a new Reading List folder called 'Research'. Then move any existing item into that folder.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, open the Reading List, mark one item as read, and report how many items remain unread.
Sign in to BBC News as david.k@test.com / TestPass123!, add the top Football article to the Reading List in a folder called 'Weekend', then remove it. Confirm it has been removed.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, open any Technology article and post the comment 'Great explainer, thanks for the depth.' Verify the comment shows up under your name.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, open any Business article, and post a comment of at least 30 characters expressing your view on the story.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123!, open a Health article, post a comment, then delete it from your own comment list.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, open a Science article, find an existing top-level comment, and post a reply to it.
Sign in to BBC News as david.k@test.com / TestPass123!, open any Sport article, like an existing comment, and report the new like count.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123! and open the Subscriptions page. List every topic you are currently subscribed to and the frequency for each.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123! and subscribe to the topic 'Cricket' at daily frequency. Confirm the new subscription appears on the Subscriptions page.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123!, change the frequency of any existing topic subscription to 'weekly', and save.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, delete any one of your topic subscriptions, and report how many subscriptions remain.
Sign in to BBC News as david.k@test.com / TestPass123! and subscribe to three new topics of your choice. Confirm all three appear on the Subscriptions page.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123! and create a new digest titled 'Monday AI Brief' that includes at least three Technology articles. Confirm the digest is created.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, open an existing digest and report its digest number, status, and number of articles.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123!, cancel one of your pending digests, and verify the status changes to 'cancelled'.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, resend a previously sent digest to your account email. Confirm the page reports a successful resend.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, open a digest detail page, and download the digest. Report the filename of the downloaded file.
Sign in to BBC News as david.k@test.com / TestPass123! and create a new digest containing two Sport articles. Verify the digest appears in your account dashboard.
Without signing in, open the Bookmarks page on BBC News. Report what the site does (which page you are redirected to, and what message is shown).
On BBC News, find an article in the Africa section, copy its source URL from the article page, and verify the URL points to bbc.com.
On BBC News, open the Markets page under Business and list which company tickers or indices are referenced.
Open BBC News, navigate to the Weather page from the masthead, and report which UK city's weather is featured at the top.
Open BBC News and use the topic page for 'AI' to list the headlines of the first three articles tagged with this topic.
Open BBC News and use the topic page for 'Climate change' to report how many articles are tagged with this topic.
Open the Culture section on BBC News, then navigate to the Film & TV sub-section, and report the headline of the latest article.
On BBC News, open the Audio section, then the Podcasts sub-section, and list two podcast titles.
Search BBC News for 'election' and from the results filter to the Politics section. Report how many results remain.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123! and from the Bookmarks page, sort or scan the list and report the section labels of the bookmarked items.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123!, post a comment on a Health article, then open the article's comment list and report the total number of comments shown.
Sign in to BBC News as david.k@test.com / TestPass123!, bookmark a Football article, add it to a Reading List folder called 'Weekend', then visit both pages and confirm the article appears in both.

Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, subscribe to the topic 'Quantum Computing' at weekly frequency, then create a digest titled 'Quantum Weekly' that contains two Technology articles.
On BBC News, browse to the Earth section, identify the most-viewed article in the Natural Wonders sub-section, and report its headline and view count.
On BBC News, find an article whose headline contains the word 'OpenAI'. Open it and report the published date, reading time, and section.
On BBC News, open the index page and count how many top-level navigation links appear in the global navigation bar at the top of the page.
On BBC News, click into the search page with no query. Report the placeholder text shown in the search box and any suggested popular searches.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, then visit the account dashboard and report the counts shown for reading list, bookmarks, and subscriptions.
Sign in to BBC News as bob.c@test.com / TestPass123!, change your bio to 'Markets watcher.' on the account edit page, save it, and confirm the new bio shows on your account page.
On BBC News, open any article and locate the comment form. Report whether the form is visible to signed-out users, and if so, what action it triggers when submitted.
On BBC News, visit the footer and follow the 'About the BBC' link. From the resulting page, report the year the BBC News Online service launched, if listed.
Open BBC News, navigate to the Innovation > Science section, find an article about astronomy or space, and summarise its key finding in one sentence.
Sign in to BBC News as alice.j@test.com / TestPass123!, then visit the Topic page for 'AI' from your subscriptions list. Report the first three article headlines shown on that page.
On BBC News, navigate to the Sport > Olympics section and report how many articles are listed on the first page and the headline of the top story.
Sign in to BBC News as carol.d@test.com / TestPass123!, then on the Reading List page report which folder contains the largest number of items and how many items it has.
